episode_summary: The team discuss bullet-proof tardigrades, a bizarre 1920s Chicago panic over stolen testicles, India’s celebrity tigress Machli who generated millions in tourism, and a Bournemouth church rebranding itself as “St. Mike’s.” Along the way they dive into animal resilience, rejuvenation fads, tiger lore, and modern church makeovers.

facts.fact: My fact this week is that tardy grades can survive being shot out of a gun, but they're a bit groggy afterwards.
My fact is that, in the 1920s, Chicago was hit by a miniature crime wave of testicle theft, which was nicknamed, which was nicknamed, "Gland Larsony."
Yes, my fact this week is that until 2016, the Indian government earned $10 million a year from a single tiger, pretty impressive tiger.
My fact this week is that St. Michael's Church in Bournemouth has been renamed St. Mike's to make it seem younger and cooler.
